text.title: ML/AI Decision Framework Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from a fresh AI-feature brief to a committed decision record.

step.name: scope-the-feature
step.input: Product brief; input/output shape; quality SLO; budget envelope.
step.action: Capture feature name, expected request volume per month, irreversible-error class (Y/N), latency SLO.
step.output: scope row in the decision record draft.

step.name: inventory-the-data
step.action: List data sources. Tag each: private/public, change_frequency (never/monthly/weekly/daily/hourly), citations_required, corpus_size. Classify specialization_class as none, behavioral, or factual.

step.name: choose-the-approach
step.action: Walk the approach branch of content/06-decision-tree.xml. If RAG triggers fire → approach=rag. If FT triggers fire AND no factual specialization AND volume ≥1M/month with ROI written → approach=fine-tune. Otherwise approach=prompt. Hybrid only when both behavioral AND fact-changing.
step.decision-gate: If approach=fine-tune AND specialization_class=factual → STOP, no-fine-tune-for-factual rule blocks.

step.name: choose-the-model-tier
step.action: Walk the model branch. Simple extraction/classification → nano (Haiku / DeepSeek / GPT-mini). Standard generation → standard (Sonnet / GPT-4o / Gemini 2 Pro). Irreversible-error OR deep multi-step reasoning → frontier (Opus / GPT-frontier).

step.name: commit-with-quarterly-review
step.action: Set quarterly_review_due = decision_date + 90 days. Run scripts/validate-decision-framework.py. Commit to docs/decisions/ alongside the feature spec.
step.output: Validated dr-* record; calendar entry for the review date.

text.reference: Markdown skeleton for the decision record.
Router config snippet for complexity-routing implementation.
